page: Amazon Web Services c7a.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. However, multi-core and memory-bound metrics show limitations. Stress-ng multi-core performance is average, while Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is weak, though large block read and write bandwidths maintain average tiers. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing are strong, but software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations are weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7a.large offers a strong single-core CPU profile within a lightweight, entry-level resource footprint. The primary tradeoff is the low memory capacity of 4.0 GB and the lack of local storage, which restricts its utility for memory-intensive or I/O-heavy applications. It provides a cost-effective entry point for workloads that require high single-threaded compute power without the overhead of larger multi-core instances.

This instance is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic LLM inference using small models, where it achieves average prompt processing and text generation speeds. It is less suitable for heavy database operations, parallel software compilation, or memory-intensive caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core benchmarks, and strong single-threaded text processing and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, with multi-core Geekbench and Passmark scores falling in the bottom 25%. Qualitatively, the c7a.large provides a cost-effective option for compute-heavy, single-threaded workloads that do not require large memory footprints or local storage. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and small-scale model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor, 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a Nitro hypervisor, delivering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, offering strong single-core speed.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processing and DDR5 memory for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 4.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and light web serving
